REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RECEIVER'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. PURSUANT TO THIS COURT'S ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER, DATED SEPTEMBER 13, 2021, THE RECEIVER SHALL HAVE THE POWER AND THE RESPONSIBILITY TO "TAKE FULL AND COMPLETE POSSESSION AND CONTROL OF THE [SUBJECT] PROPERTY . . . [T]O TEMPORARILY OR PERMANENTLY RELOCATE THE PROPERTY'S OCCUPANTS [] IF NECESSARY TO IMPLEMENT THE RECEIVERSHIP PLAN, AS DETERMINED BY THE RECEIVER IN ITS SOLE AND ABSOLUTE DISCRETION." ACCORDINGLY, PER THE SEPTEMBER 13, 2021 ORDER, RECEIVER MAY TEMPORARILY VACATE AND DISPOSSESS DEFENDANT FROM THE SUBJECT PROPERTY FOR THE AMOUNT OF TIME THAT IS NECESSARY FOR HIM TO REMEDIATE THAT PROPERTY'S NUMEROUS VIOLATIONS AND DEFICIENCIES. Notice Of Motion For Default Judgment Against Defendant Eileen L. Walsh
Off Calendar Jul-07-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendant Eileen L. Walsh.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:24 a.m., adjourning at 11:48 a.m. and reconvening at 11:54 a.m.

Megan E. Ryan, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco, a municipal corporation, and the People of the State of California, by and through Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco.
Donal Duffy, retired Inspector for the San Francisco Department of Building Inspection, Adaku Ude, Program Manager for the San Francisco Department of Public Health, and Captain Mary Tse of the San Francisco Fire Department, were placed under oath and testified.
Bryan Delgado, Esq. appeared for defendant-in-intervention Newrez LLC, dba Shellpoint Mortgage Serving.
Counsel and the witnesses all appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Eileen L. Walsh.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Violations of State Housing Law, Public Nuisance and Violations of the San Francisco Building, Housing, Fire and Health Codes was filed on February 19, 2021, and personally served on defendant Eileen L. Walsh on March 11, 2021.
Lis Pendens was filed on May 13, 2021.
Default as to defendant Eileen L. Walsh was entered on May 27, 2021.
Order granting motion to intervene by NewrezLLC, dba Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing, was filed on November 16, 2021.
Answer-in-Intervention by Newrez LLC was filed on November 23, 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed on March 7, 2022.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco, a municipal corporation, and the People of the State of California, by and through Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco, and against the defaulted defendant Eileen L. Walsh, as follows:

Civil Penalties pursuant to San Francisco Building Code Section 103A - $10,000.00.
Civil Penalties pursuant to San Francisco Housing Code Section 204(c)(2) - $7,000.00.
Civil Penalties pursuant to San Francisco Plumbing Code Section 106.3 - $1,000.00.
Civil Penalties pursuant to San Francisco Fire Code Section 110.4.4 - $4,000.00.
Civil Penalties pursuant to San Francisco Health Code Section 600 - $3,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $70,570.50.
Costs - $148.77.

Total - $95,719.27.

Additionally, defendant Eileen L. Walsh is restrained and enjoined, directly or indirectly through an agent, officer, manager, representative, employee or any else acting on defendant Walsh's behalf, from doing any of the following:
(a) Maintaining the property (200 Judson Avenue, San Francisco, CA) in such a manner and condition as to constitute a public nuisance as defined by Civil Code Sections 3479 and 3480 and the San Francisco Housing, Building, Plumbing Electrical, Fire and Health Codes; and
(b) Maintaining the property in such a manner and condition as to violate the State Housing Law, codified as California Health and Safety Code Sections 17910-17998.3.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 12:15 p.m.
